p.seg: To all Holy Mother Church's sons intending to examine the present writing, Roger, by the grace of God a humble servant of the church of St Andrew, [sends] greeting in the Lord.

to have given and granted and by the present charter to have made firm to William son of Adam of Nevay, at the presentation of the same Adam, the church of [Meathie] Lour with all its just pertinents in free, pure, and perpetual alms. Wherefore I will and firmly instruct that the same William hold and possess the aforenamed church freely, quitly, and honourably, just as any parson most freely, quitly, and honourably holds and possesses his church in our diocese. Reserving episcopal rights.

As witnesses: Henry, abbot of Arbroath; Thomas, prior of St Andrews; Archibald, abbot of Scone; Hugh, prior of Restenneth; Master Ranulf, official; Master Isaac; Master William of Lyne; Richard and Richard, chaplains; John of London; Richard le Megre; William Wyville; John of Bearford; Hugh the steward; Ralph, priest of Glamis; Baldwin the Scot; John, dean of Angus; Simon, dean of Perth; John, dean of Kinghorn; and a great many others.
